title: EEG: Simon Conflict in Parkinson's
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 28 Parkinson's disease patients and 28 matched controls performing a Simon conflict task with cost of conflict reinforcement manipulation. Parkinson's patients were tested twice within one week under both ON and OFF medication conditions, while controls participated in a single session. The study investigates neural correlates of conflict processing and decision-making in Parkinson's disease using event-related potentials.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during a Simon conflict task adapted from established protocols. Parkinson's disease patients completed two sessions separated by one week, tested under both ON and OFF medication states. Control subjects completed a single session. Accelerometer data (X, Y, Z dimensions) were simultaneously recorded from the most tremor-affected hand in Parkinson's patients. Task stimuli were presented using MATLAB programming language.
